home.subtitle: Analyze your Prakriti body constitution, curate personalized seasonal meal schedules, cross-check incompatible food combinations, and seek 24/7 AI-guided wellness tips.

philosophy.title: Discover Our Core Wellness Engines
philosophy.prakritiDesc1: In Ayurvedic medicine, Prakriti defines your body constitution—a blueprint of physical characteristics, metabolic traits, and emotional tendencies. Maintaining balance between the three primary bio-energies (Doshas: Vata, Pitta, Kapha) is key to preventative wellness.
philosophy.prakritiDesc2: AyurNutriCare helps you identify this balance, matching modern vitamins/minerals with Ayurvedic tastes (Rasa), energy potencies (Virya), and seasonal rhythms to keep you aligned.

features.prakritiDesc: Find your dominant Dosha (Vata, Pitta, or Kapha) using our animated multi-step mind/body quiz.
features.dietDesc: Generate meal recommendations tailored to your Dosha type and the current season (Ritu Charya).
features.botDesc: Consult AyurBot 24/7 for meal rationales, macronutrient lookups, and Ayurvedic recipe ideation.
features.checkerDesc: Avoid toxic dietary pairings like Milk & Fish or equal parts Honey & Ghee using our real-time checker.

testimonials.title: Wellness Testimonials
testimonials.subtitle: What our members say
testimonials.user1Text: "Being a Vata type, my digestion was always erratic. AyurNutriCare's warm khichdi meal recommendations during Vasanta season saved my energy levels completely."
testimonials.user2Text: "The Viruddha Ahara compatibility checker is an absolute game-changer. I didn't realize my daily morning banana milkshakes were triggering my chronic acid reflux!"
testimonials.user3Text: "AyurBot acts as my virtual pocket doctor. Whenever I dine out, I ask it for ingredient compatibility profiles, helping me keep my Kapha energy balanced."

checker.searchPlaceholder: Type a food (e.g. Milk, Fish, Banana, Curd)...

checker.selectMinIngredients: Select at least 2 ingredients to see the compatibility evaluation.
checker.compatibleDesc: All added ingredients are compatible and can be safely cooked or eaten together in a single meal.
checker.tipText: 💡 Tip: Foods of opposite thermal properties (Veerya: e.g. heating Honey and cooling Milk) or digestibilities should be taken at least 2 hours apart to protect your digestive fire.

quiz.alertUnanswered: Please answer all questions before submitting.
quiz.vataInfo: Vata types have a predominant air/space energy. They are creative, energetic, and dry-natured. Diet-wise, they need warm, heavy, moist, and grounding foods (like grains, warm soups, and healthy oils) to combat cold and dry qualities.
quiz.pittaInfo: Pitta types are ruled by the fire/water element. They are focused, analytical, warm, and highly metabolic. They require sweet, cooling, and calming ingredients (like sweet fruits, milk, ghee, and leafy greens) to neutralize digestive acidity and high body heat.
quiz.kaphaInfo: Kapha types have an earth/water dominance. They are strong, steady, loving, and patient, but metabolically slow. They require light, dry, warm, and stimulating foods (like legumes, millets, ginger, and hot warming spices) to offset heavy, damp tendencies.
quiz.dualInfo: Your constitution represents a rich combination of dual elements. You possess traits of multiple bio-energies. Focus on eating foods that balance your primary Doshas, especially focusing on local seasonal schedules!

questions.text: Describe your physical structure and bone frame:

options.text: Thin, bony, tall or very short, with prominent joints and difficulty gaining weight.
Medium build, athletic, well-proportioned, and easy to maintain weight.
Broad, sturdy, large-boned, with a tendency to gain weight easily and lose it slowly.

questions.text: How does your skin look and feel most of the time?

options.text: Dry, rough, cool to touch, and prone to cracking or ashiness.
Warm, sensitive, reddish/fair complexion, prone to acne, freckles, or sunburn.
Thick, smooth, soft, slightly oily, cool, and clear complexion.

questions.text: Describe your hair quality and texture:

options.text: Dry, curly or frizzy, brittle, coarse, and dark brown or black.
Fine, straight, silky, thin, reddish/blonde, or prone to early graying/thinning.
Thick, wavy, abundant, oily, strong, and dark in color.

questions.text: How does your stomach behave after meals?

options.text: Irregular and erratic; prone to gas, bloating, and constipation.
Strong and fast; digests everything easily, but prone to acidity, heartburn, or loose stools.
Slow and heavy; feels full for a long time, digestion is sluggish, but regular bowels.
Light, interrupted, easily disturbed by noise, and average 5-6 hours.
Moderate and sound; can sleep easily, average 7-8 hours, and feel rested.
Deep, heavy, long; hard to wake up in the morning, average 8-10 hours, and prone to sleepiness.

questions.text: How does your mind process information?

options.text: Quick-thinking, creative, talks fast, but gets distracted easily and has a restless mind.
Sharp, focused, logical, highly organized, quick-witted, and competitive.
Calm, steady, patient, slow to learn but has excellent long-term memory.

questions.text: How do you react to stressful situations?

options.text: Prone to anxiety, worry, fear, and feeling overwhelmed or nervous.
Prone to anger, irritation, impatience, and aggressive behavior.
Calm, loving, composed, stable, but can become lazy, attached, or complacent.

questions.text: Describe your physical energy and stamina:

options.text: Hyperactive or bursts of high energy, but tires out very quickly and needs frequent rest.
Moderate energy, driven, determined, and will push through fatigue to finish a goal.
Steady, enduring stamina; slow to start but can work long hours without tiring.

questions.text: How is your appetite throughout the day?

options.text: Variable; I forget to eat, or get hungry at irregular times. Digestion varies.
Intense and sharp; I cannot skip meals without feeling angry ("hangry") or weak.
Moderate and steady; I can easily skip a meal without feeling weak or irritated.

questions.text: Which weather is most uncomfortable for you?

options.text: Cold, windy, and dry weather makes me feel stiff and uncomfortable.
Hot, sunny, and humid weather makes me feel hot, sweat excessively, and get irritated.
Cold, damp, cloudy, and rainy weather makes me feel heavy, congested, and lazy.

morningRoutine.desc: Warm water with a teaspoon of Honey
morningRoutine.note: Grounding and stimulates Agni (digestive fire) gently without heating.

breakfast.desc: Warm wheat porridge cooked in Ghee with cinnamon and sliced almonds.
breakfast.note: Heavy, sweet, and oily qualities balance Vata's light, dry traits.

midMorningSnack.note: Sweet and astringent, hydrates Vata tissues.

lunch.desc: Moong Dal Khichdi cooked in Ghee with fresh Ginger, cumin, coriander.
lunch.note: Perfect, easy-to-digest protein that pacifies Vata.

eveningSnack.note: Nourishing fats support nervous system stability.

dinner.desc: Stewed Bottle Gourd curry with white basmati rice.
dinner.note: Cooked vegetables are easy for Vata's erratic digestion to handle.

bedtime.desc: Warm organic Milk with a pinch of Cardamom
bedtime.note: Promotes deep restful sleep and soothes dry nerves.

morningRoutine.desc: Fresh coconut water or warm milk
morningRoutine.note: Cooling start to neutralize excessive stomach acidity.

breakfast.desc: Rice Poha tempered with mild turmeric, cumin, and fresh coriander.
breakfast.note: Sweet, cooling, and moderate carbs pacify Pitta fire.

midMorningSnack.desc: Amla juice or fresh sweet fruits.

lunch.desc: Basmati rice, light moong dal, and steamed spinach with ghee.

eveningSnack.note: Astringent and cooling taste balances internal heat.

dinner.desc: Whole wheat flatbread (Chapati) with bottle gourd subji.
dinner.note: Mild, sweet, grounding properties that cool the liver.

bedtime.desc: Warm milk with cardamom and raw sugar.
bedtime.note: Cooling post-digestive effect reduces hot dreams.

morningRoutine.desc: Warm water with fresh Ginger extract and Honey
morningRoutine.note: Pungent and heating qualities scrape away sluggish mucus.

breakfast.desc: Methi (fenugreek) paratha cooked dry, or ragi malt.
breakfast.note: Bitter, dry, and light. Prevents fluid retention.

lunch.desc: Chana dal subji with sautéed Bitter Gourd and turmeric.
lunch.note: Highly astringent and light, keeping Kapha active and light.

eveningSnack.note: Honey is the best sweetener to pacify Kapha.

dinner.desc: Clear Moong Dal soup with spinach and cracked black pepper.
dinner.note: Warm, spicy, and light dinner that prevents weight gain.

bedtime.note: Clears the respiratory tract during Kapha hours.
